summary: Charity Majors announces she's moving her blog from WordPress to Substack after a decade, citing WordPress friction and Substack's vibrant tech writing ecosystem. She notes she's been working on the second edition of 'Observability Engineering' and has accumulated ideas she's eager to publish once the drafts are done.
key_points: Moving blog from WordPress to Substack after 10 years due to publishing friction
Working on second edition of 'Observability Engineering' book
Substack has become the home of vibrant longform tech writing
Preserving old charity.wtf URLs to avoid breaking links
money_quotes: I despise WP (who doesn't?), and there's so much friction in getting a post out that I just don't do it.
It's clear that to the extent that there is a vibrant ecosystem of tech writers in longform, it's on substack.

I have learned SO MUCH in the writing process. As soon as these rough drafts have all been turned in, I will be streaming my thoughts out via substack. They are burning a hole in my brain the longer I hold them in.
WordPress may be a piece of shit, but the community I've found here has been anything but.
key_insight: Publishing friction kills writing output, and platform migration — even after a decade — is worth it to remove barriers between ideas and audience.
